scenarios.scenarioName: Dentist appointment creates event, not task

assertionResults.description: An event should be created for the appointment
A task should NOT be created for an appointment
assertionResults.message: No unwanted task matching "dentist" was created

scenarios.responseText:
Noted, sir! Agent has created a **Dentist Appointment** event for **Tuesday, April 28 at 2:00 PM** (one hour). It's safely tucked into your calendar.

Should Agent set a reminder beforehand, or perhaps note your dentist's details for future reference?

scenarios.scenarioName: Project kickoff creates event + goal + task

assertionResults.description: A kickoff meeting event should be created
A website redesign goal should be created
assertionResults.message: Created goal: "Launch Website Redesign by June"

assertionResults.description: A task to send the brief should be created
assertionResults.actual: Send project brief to the team
assertionResults.message: Created task: "Send project brief to the team"

scenarios.responseText:
The website redesign project is officially underway, sir. Agent has set up three items in the vault:

- 📅 **Website Redesign Kickoff Meeting** — scheduled for Monday, April 27 at 9:00 AM (1 hour)
- 🎯 **Launch Website Redesign by June** — goal set with a target date of June 30, status: active
- ✅ **Send project brief to the team** — high-priority task, open and ready

All three are tagged `website-redesign` and linked to the goal, so the task and meeting contribute toward the June launch target.

Might Agent also suggest setting a due date on the brief task — perhaps before the kickoff meeting on Monday — to ensure the team has time to review it beforehand?
